selected_important_turn.text: Great, hey, I'll make you an offer, I'll keep 3 firewood, 3 food and...
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn sharply shifts the negotiation from cooperative information-sharing to a competitive, zero-sum claim. The opponent had just shared environmental information (hot days, cold nights) that implied needs; by immediately claiming all firewood and food, you lock in a self-favoring split and push the interaction toward division rather than joint problem-solving.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is unnecessarily aggressive relative to the cooperative context; it ignores the opponent's implicit needs (water priority) and forecloses integrative trade opportunities. It's consequential because it frames the allocation as a claim rather than an exchange, increasing the risk of mistrust or negotiation breakdown. Yet it's still improvable — you can preserve your interest in firewood while opening trades that create value for both sides.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from an outright claim to an integrative, information-seeking or conditional offer: acknowledge the other's conditions and either ask about their highest needs or propose a trade (e.g., offer to give up some food or water in exchange for more firewood, or propose a split that reflects different item priorities). Use conditional language or invite preferences before locking quantities.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — creating tradeoffs (e.g., water for firewood) can expand value compared with a hard claim
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — a negotiated trade that reflects both parties' priorities will feel fairer than a one-sided claim
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — by opening a collaborative bargaining frame you reduce resistance and make agreement smoother
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — being less greedy and more consultative builds trust and rapport

value2reason.Low: I can stay hydrated so I can do the chores
value2reason.Medium: To stay active and energized and not to lose my face
value2reason.High: To keep me warm in low temperatures

value2reason.Low: I will need the extra food to feed the extra people who are coming with me.
value2reason.Medium: It has been really hot and I'll need to keep hydrated while hiking
value2reason.High: It has been unsually cold at night where I'm camping.

dialogue.text: Hi there!  I'm so excited to be going camping this weekend.  I haven't been able to do anything because of the pandemic.  Do you have any plans for the weekend?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: No, I don't have any plans for this weekend. Can we camp together?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: If you want to tag along, sure.  lol  I'll be heading to the mountains so it's going to be hot during the day and cold during the night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Great, hey, I'll make you an offer, I'll keep 3 firewood, 3 food and...
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand the weather conditions. Since firewood is my top priority, I’d like to propose a trade. If you need more food, I can give you one package of food for one package of firewood. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m interested in the trade, but I need more firewood than food. How about I give you two food packages for one firewood package? That way I get more firewood while you get some food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to adjust. How about I give you two food packages for one firewood package, and you give me one water package? It balances our priorities and ensures we both have essentials.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let me confirm: you’d get two food packages and one water package, while I give you one firewood package. Is that acceptable?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let me finalize the deal. I’ll take two food packages and one water package, and you take one firewood package. That splits the supplies fairly based on our priorities. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
